user_query:
I’m finalizing a release and need you to fact-check my claims and sync the code with what’s actually enabled. Use these files:
- config/game.yaml
- src/constants.py
- notes/RELEASE_NOTES.md

Requirements:
1) Version sync: Parse the release version from the first header in notes/RELEASE_NOTES.md that looks like "Release vX.Y.Z". Update src/constants.py so VERSION is exactly "X.Y.Z" (strip the leading "v").

2) Feature flag sync: Treat config/game.yaml as the source of truth for features. Update FEATURE_FLAGS in src/constants.py to match exactly the keys and boolean values under features in config/game.yaml. Remove any keys not present there. Do not include experimental flags in FEATURE_FLAGS.

3) Claim verification: Compare the "Highlights" in notes/RELEASE_NOTES.md against config/game.yaml.
- Identify which claimed stable features are disabled in config (claimed_but_disabled).
- Identify which enabled features (config.features == true) were not claimed in the notes (enabled_but_unclaimed).
- Also capture which experimental items (under config.experimental == true) were claimed as experimental. When matching feature names, normalize snake_case keys to title/space form (e.g., "photo_mode" -> "Photo Mode") and compare case-insensitively.

4) Report: Write output/check_report.json with these fields:
- release_version: string
- feature_flags_before: object (dict from src/constants.py before changes)
- feature_flags_after: object (dict after sync)
- claimed_stable: array of strings
- claimed_experimental: array of strings
- claimed_but_disabled: array of strings
- enabled_but_unclaimed: array of strings
- files_updated: array of file paths you modified

5) Rewrite notes: Create output/RELEASE_NOTES_CLEAN.md.
- Keep the original title line exactly as in the source (including the quoted tagline).
- Replace the "Highlights" section with bullet points for only the enabled stable features (config.features == true). Use Title Case names derived from the snake_case keys and add one short sentence per bullet in a dry, dark-humor tone (PG-13; no profanity).
- Add an "Experimental" section listing items with true under config.experimental in the same style.
- Replace the existing "Fixes" block with a single concise sentence that captures the gist of the original fixes.
- Do not mention any feature that is false in config.features.

Save changes to src/constants.py in place and create the files under output/. Create any missing directories as needed. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
